pod restarting: Check pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and inspect events using 'kubectl describe pod <pod-name>'.
high cpu: Use 'top' or 'htop' to identify the process consuming CPU.
disk full: Run 'df -h' to check disk usage and remove unnecessary files.
service down: Check service status using 'systemctl status <service>' and restart if necessary.
kubernetes pod pending: Check node resources using 'kubectl describe pod <pod-name>' and verify scheduler events.
container crashloopbackoff: Inspect container logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and check restart count.
memory high: Run 'free -m' or 'top' to check memory usage and identify memory consuming processes.
node not ready: Run 'kubectl describe node <node-name>' and check kubelet status.
image pull error: Verify image name and registry access using 'kubectl describe pod'.
port already in use: Run 'netstat -tulnp' or 'lsof -i:<port>' to identify the process using the port.
application not responding: Check application logs and verify service health endpoints.
database connection issue: Verify database credentials, network connectivity, and DB service status.
dns resolution issue: Use 'nslookup <domain>' or 'dig <domain>' to verify DNS resolution.
high load average: Use 'uptime' or 'top' to check system load and running processes.
deployment failed: Check rollout status using 'kubectl rollout status deployment <deployment-name>'.
pod evicted: Check node memory and disk pressure conditions.
kubernetes service not reachable: Verify service endpoints using 'kubectl get endpoints'.
container not starting: Inspect container logs and check Dockerfile configuration.
permission denied: Verify file permissions using 'ls -l' and adjust using 'chmod'.
file system read only: Check disk errors and remount file system if required.
process not running: Use 'ps aux | grep <process>' to verify running processes.
docker build failed: Check Dockerfile syntax and build logs.
node disk pressure: Check disk usage on the node and clean up unused images or logs.
log file too large: Rotate logs or clean unnecessary log files.
application latency: Check service response time and backend dependencies.
container networking issue: Verify network policies and service endpoints.
helm deployment error: Run 'helm status <release-name>' and check helm logs.
api server not responding: Check kube-apiserver logs and cluster health.
